change_summary: Define the template operator model around a small human-facing command surface, comparative-strength dispatch, and a clear boundary between facade UX, agent-native execution, and the governed CLI/MCP core.
verification_commands: rg -n 'agent next|agent do <ticket>|Facade owns UX|coord/scripts/agent|Comparative-Advantage Dispatch|capability registry' coord/GOVERNANCE_ARCHITECTURE.md coord/AGENT_PATHS.md coord/VERB_CONTRACT.md coord/AGENT_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY.md
critical_invariants: The human-facing operator model stays small while lifecycle correctness remains in the shared governance core.
Agent-native wrappers and ecosystems remain adapters above the same canonical board, lock, evidence, and recovery model.
requirement_closure: Ticket ask: Define the governed operator model: a small human-facing command surface, mandate-based dispatch, a shared CLI/MCP core, and the boundary between facade UX, agent-native execution, and canonical governance state.

Implemented: Defined the target-state governance architecture, the shared/native agent path model, the neutral verb contract, and the starter capability-registry baseline for Claude, Codex, and Gemini.
Not implemented: The neutral facade executable and runtime registry automation are not built yet; those remain follow-on implementation work under GOV-009 and GOV-010.

self_review_cycles.diff: Defined the operator-model contract across governance architecture, native-path guidance, and the neutral verb surface, then clarified the current-state versus target-state boundary for the facade.
risks: The template could overstate unimplemented facade behavior if target-state and current-state are not separated clearly.
Different agent shims could drift if the shared operator model is not described once at the architecture and path-selection levels.
self_review_cycles.verification: rg -n 'Current Implementation Boundary|agent next|coord/scripts/agent|Neutral Verb Contract' coord/GOVERNANCE_ARCHITECTURE.md coord/AGENT_PATHS.md coord/VERB_CONTRACT.md

self_review_cycles.diff: Kept governance correctness in the shared CLI/MCP core and explicitly left agent-native wrappers as adapters so provider-specific paths cannot become shadow authorities.
risks: Prompt or wrapper guidance could silently take over lifecycle authority if the boundary with the governed core is vague.
Comparative-strength dispatch could flatten into agent favoritism instead of an explicit
self_review_cycles.verification: rg -n 'Engine owns correctness|Agent owns cognition|CLI and MCP harmonize the core|Native ecosystems stay native|Dispatch Rule' coord/GOVERNANCE_ARCHITECTURE.md coord/AGENT_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY.md

self_review_cycles.diff: Turned the capability registry into a usable template baseline by replacing blank placeholder rows with actual starter entries for Claude, Codex, and Gemini, while preserving later project refinement.
risks: A placeholder-only registry would make comparative-strength dispatch nominal instead of actionable.
Over-specifying per-project truth in the template would create a false sense of provider capability certainty.

rollback_strategy: Revert the operator-model design docs to the previous scaffold language if the template decides not to standardize the facade, dispatch, and capability-registry contract at the governance level.

bootstrap_risk.rollback_or_disable: feature flag off by default; job can be rerun from checkpoint
